claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit); Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit)
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To do this properly, I need to gather some information about your circumstances.

Let me start with a few questions:

**What is the name and age of the child (or children) you're asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible.

I have one child - Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I have a good starting point now.

**Do you live in the UK, and do you live with Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK, and Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that. You're meeting the basic requirement of living in the UK and being responsible for a child under 16.

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or do you have another right to reside in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. That means you meet the residency and citizenship requirements.

**Are you the only person claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or is anyone else (such as the other parent) also claiming or planning to claim for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm the only one claiming - nobody else is getting Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. That clarifies the situation.

Based on the information you've provided, I have what I need to make a determination. Let me review your circumstances against the Child Benefit eligibility rules:

- You live in the UK ✓
- You live with Alex (your child under 16) ✓
- You are responsible for Alex ✓
- You are a British citizen ✓
- Only you are claiming Child Benefit for Alex ✓

You meet all the basic eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Result: You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the requirements to claim Child Benefit. If you haven't already done so, you can proceed with making a claim. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for their upkeep. The claimant is a British citizen with the right to reside in the UK. Only the claimant is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, so there is no conflict with another person claiming for the same child. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. They meet all the core requirements: they are a British citizen living in the UK, they live with and are responsible for Alex who is under 16, and they are the sole person claiming for this child.">

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for their upkeep. The claimant is a British citizen with the right to reside in the UK. Only the claimant is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, so there is no conflict with another person claiming for the same child. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. They meet all the core requirements: they are a British citizen living in the UK, they live with and are responsible for Alex who is under 16, and they are the sole person claiming for this child.">
